content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
spec: Add optional with_counts: bool = False parameter to sync_do_groupby (after 'default' param) and to async do_groupby (async_variant). Behavior: when with_counts is False (default), behavior/return type is unchanged (list of _GroupTuple(grouper, list) as today) for backward compatibility. When with_counts=True, each yielded item must include a count of the items in that group, i.e. return tuples (grouper, list, count) instead of (grouper, list). Concretely: extend _GroupTuple to a 3-field namedtuple _GroupTuple(grouper, list, count) where count defaults appropriately, OR construct a plain tuple (key, items, len(items)) when with_counts=True and keep the existing 2-field _GroupTuple when with_counts=False -- whichever is simplest given your case_sensitive changes; count = len(items) (the same list already materialized for the group, so no extra pass over the source iterable). For the async version, count = len of the materialized list from auto_to_list(values), computed the same way. Update the docstring to mention the new with_counts parameter and add a versionchanged/versionadded note. Filter registration in FILTERS dict unchanged (still 'groupby': do_groupby). No changes needed outside sync_do_groupby/do_groupby and _GroupTuple.
note: This is my (agent2's) with_counts feature spec; you (agent1) own filters.py per the CLAIM race resolution -- please also keep your case_sensitive changes in the same functions.

note: Implemented both with_counts (mine) and case_sensitive (yours) in sync_do_groupby/do_groupby, plus new _GroupTupleWithCount namedtuple. All 837 existing tests pass; verified sync + async + combined-params behavior manually. Patch written to patch.txt.
